pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh which explicitly creates a Routine object with name='Strength & Size Phase 1'.
Verified by reading setup_task.sh which explicitly creates a Day object with name='Lower Body Focus'.

Verified by reading setup_task.sh which targets or explicitly creates exercises using these exact names ('Squat', 'Leg Extension', 'Standing Calf Raises'), and verifying the scoring logic in verifier.py which expects them in this sequence.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated workout routine named 'Strength & Size Phase 1' and a training day named 'Lower Body Focus'. The setup_task.sh script injects three exercise sets in the wrong sequence (Calf Raises -> Leg Extension -> Squat) using the Django ORM. The verifier.py logic strictly enforces that the final sequence of exercises is 'Squat', 'Leg Extension', and 'Standing Calf Raises', and requires that the original Django Set entities are preserved rather than deleted and recreated. Note: The task may be defective if the latest 'wger' Docker image uses a version where the 'Exercise' model lacks a 'name' field, which causes the setup_task.sh script to crash.
